description: Demo of ambient NPC-to-NPC conversations (US-129). A cellar tavern under a truce: two pairs of off-duty road-bandits and the keeper's people loiter, drink, and talk among THEMSELVES while you wander. Stand near a pair and watch overheard banter play out line by line in the Event Log and speech bubbles — chosen by who they are to each other, never directed at you, and only when you're close enough to hear.
customIntroduction: The Hidden Keg keeps an old rule: blades stay sheathed under its roof. Tonight that truce holds a clutch of road-bandits at two tables — boots up, cups out, grousing at each other the way tired men do — while Bram works the counter and a wary villager nurses a drink in the corner. Nobody's looking for trouble. You're free to drift between the tables and listen.
customContext:
DEMO — AMBIENT CONVERSATIONS (US-129). This is a NON-COMBAT loitering scene that showcases NPCs talking to EACH OTHER, not to the player. The encounter-start triggers set a tavern truce (party↔bandits relation neutralised) so the road-bandits spawn and stay NEUTRAL — do NOT start combat, do NOT flip anyone to enemy unless the player forces it. Keep everyone calm.

HOW IT WORKS (engine-driven — don't fake it): while the player explores, the world tick pairs up idle, calm, mutually-visible NPCs the player can witness (within ~8 tiles + line of sight) and plays a short authored exchange between them, one line per ~6 seconds, surfaced as dimmed 'ambient' Event Log lines + speech bubbles. The two bandit pairs share a faction, so they read as FRIENDLY and trade the 'bandits_grumble' banter (worn boots, the noose, dreams of farming). Selection is by relationship + faction + time of day; it stops the instant anything happens.

WHAT TO DEMONSTRATE — let it happen, then narrate around it:
1. LOITER near a bandit pair and overheard grumbling appears on its own, line by line. Walk away and out of earshot and it falls silent — the chatter only plays where the player can witness it.
2. The lines depend on WHO is talking: same-faction bandits gossip as friends; a barbed-rivalry pack exists for enemies who aren't fighting (not staged here — needs a grudge), and an evening/night villager-rumor pack only surfaces after dusk.
3. If the player ASKS what someone said, you (the GM) can see the recent overheard lines in your CURRENT STATE under OVERHEARD and answer naturally.
4. The truce is the point — if the player draws steel or provokes a bandit, you may set_disposition that bandit to enemy and trigger_combat; banter stops the moment a fight starts.
objective: Wander the tavern and eavesdrop. Watch the regulars talk among themselves — and notice the chatter only plays where you can hear it.

then.message: DEMO: a tavern truce holds — the bandits won't fight. Drift between the tables and LOITER near a pair: their grumbling plays out on its own, line by line (dimmed 'overheard' lines + speech bubbles), but only while you're close enough to hear. Step away and it goes quiet. The banter is chosen by who they are to each other; ask the GM what someone said and it'll know.
